answer:
**The work is *San Hyan Mahajnana* (Unknown author, Medieval Kawi prose), Chapter 1.**[^1]

This text is framed as guru-to-disciple instruction, with the 'revered Kumāra' (also called 'revered son') as the questioner addressing the Lord Guru.[^2]

The requested verse-to-verse mapping of the four states (*jāgrat* = waking; *svapna* = dream; *suṣupti* = deep sleep; *tūrya* = fourth) onto the four Vedic śrauta sacrifices appears at verse 48:

> *Jāgrat is the aśvamedha sacrifice; svapna is the vājapeya. Suṣupti is the puṇḍarīka, and tūrya is the rājasūya. // 48*[^3]

The immediate prose gloss that follows is:

> The state of *jāgrat* is the *aśvamedha* sacrifice. The state of *svapna* is the *vājapeya* sacrifice. The state of *suṣupti* is the *puṇḍarīka*. The state of *tūrya* is called the *rājasūya*.[^4]

[^1]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, Chapter 1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/).

[^2]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, Chapter 1, [p-2fcef0](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-2fcef0), [p-6fa695](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-6fa695).

[^3]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, Chapter 1, [p-5b852a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-5b852a).

[^4]: *San Hyan Mahajnana*, Chapter 1, [p-9b49bb](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-9b49bb).

args.query: jāgrat aśvamedha svapna vājapeya suṣupti puṇḍarīka tūrya rājasūya
"revered son" "four states" sacrifice OR yajña OR śrauta
